title: eschenbachia japonica
intro: Eschenbachia japonica, also known as Japanese Angelica, is a perennial plant species in the carrot family (Apiaceae) native to East Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Eschenbachia japonica include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve menstrual cramps, and reduce fever. The plant is often prepared as a decoction, tincture, or dried root, which can be used in herbal preparations to support overall health and well-being.
